title: macOS and iOS Support, Security, and Recovery · Stakeholder Support Mission
summary: A mixed-platform organization, application owner, data custodian, affected users, and accessibility reviewer. They need current lifecycle evidence, a reversible Windows migration, native macOS and iOS support, application and data verification, accessibility preservation, rollback, and a tested recovery handoff. Planned artifact: Deliver Multi-OS Migration and Recovery Service with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Track release, update channel, support phase, end date, extension, hardware eligibility, application support, migration, rollback, and exception owner.
Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change.
Treat Windows 10 exam knowledge as a dated legacy-support and migration case after its general support end, with current exception lookup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project Lifecycle Support
steps.instruction:
project case — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The project variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Track release, update channel, support phase, end date, extension, hardware eligibility, application support, migration, rollback, and exception owner.

steps.instruction:
project case — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The project variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Project Apple Changed Case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair. For CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Project Apple Changed Case, keep Apple Changed Case connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Apple Changed Case symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Apple Platform Security — Controls current Apple hardware, system, encryption, app, service, and device-management security boundaries.

For CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Project Apple Changed Case, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.

stimulus.title: Apple Changed Case failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Apple Changed Case condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Apple Changed Case.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Apple Changed Case
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Apple Changed Case: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The project variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Apple Changed Case.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.
